Hallucinated citations never enter storage. 2. **Withhold on recall** — every recall re-verifies cited files. If the evidence was deleted, the TTL lapsed, or the memory was reported stale, it is suppressed (and shown to you in the viewer, never silently dropped). 3. **Stale-ca {"file": {"content": " refused. Hallucinated citations never enter storage. 2. **Withhold on recall** — every recall re-verifies cited files. If the evidence was deleted, the TTL lapsed, or the memory was reported stale, it is suppressed (and shown to you in the viewer, never silently dropped). 3. **Stale-catch at change-time** — `kage pr check` (and `kage staleguard` as a pre-commit hook) leads with what your diff just broke: ```text ⚠ Your changes invalidated 15 team memories: • CI: Kage PR Check must block only on hard-stale memory — cites mcp/kernel.ts (file changed) fix: kage learn (update) | kage supersede --packet ``` And a privacy guarantee on top: wrap anything in `…` and Kage will never store it — the span is replaced with `[private]` before any packet or observation touches disk. Prove it on your own repo: `kage benchmark --trust --project .` measures hallucination rejection, stale exclusion, and live grounding — 100/100. ## The numbers - **18% faster than grep at equal correctness** on real code-navigation tasks (N=3 task suite, same agent, same model; reproduce with ` - /Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/README.md: {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/README.md", "newString": "And a privacy guarantee on top: wrap anything in `…` and Kage will never store it — the span is replaced with `[private]` before any packet or observation touches disk. The session loop takes care of itself: if the age {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/README.md", "newString": "And a privacy guarantee on top: wrap anything in `…` and Kage will never store it — the span is replaced with `[private]` before any packet or observation touches disk. The session loop takes care of itself: if the agent never captured anything, the session's observations are **auto-distilled into pending drafts** at session end (reviewed by you, never trusted blindly); the next session opens with a **"previously…" digest** (`kage resume`); the viewer streams memory events **live** as they happen; and when anything breaks, **`kage repair`** backs up, fixes, and rebuilds in one command.", "oldString": "And a privacy guarantee on top: wrap anything in `…` and Kage will never store it — the span is replaced with `[private]` before any packet or observation touches disk.", "originalFile": "
# Kage ### Verified repo knowledge for coding agents Every claim is cited against your current code — and you see exactly what it saves you. Kage rejects memory that cites files that don't exist, withholds memory whose - /Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/index.html: {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/index.html", "newString": "
The session loop

It takes care of itself.

Memory systems die when they depend on anyone remembering {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/index.html", "newString": "

The session loop

It takes care of itself.

Memory systems die when they depend on anyone remembering to use them. Kage's loop runs on hooks — capture, resume, repair — with you reviewing, not babysitting.

Auto-capture, signal-gated

Sessions that produced no explicit learnings are distilled into pending drafts at session end. A signal score filters out raw payloads and noise — junk never becomes memory, and drafts never enter trusted recall until you approve them.

"Previously…" on every start

kage resume opens each session with what you were doing, what was learned, drafts awaiting review, and a compact timeline of recent memory — ~800 tokens, injected automatically.

Memory at the moment of reading

When the agent opens a file, verified packets - /Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/index.html: {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/index.html", "newString": " Viewer

Watch memory happen, live.

The viewer leads with your Gains receipt and a live feed — packets and value events stream in as the agent works. Drill into packets, code links {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/index.html", "newString": " Viewer

Watch memory happen, live.

The viewer leads with your Gains receipt and a live feed — packets and value events stream in as the agent works. Drill into packets, code links, risks, and the review queue when you need to trust what was retrieved.

", "oldString": " Viewer

Inspect what agents recall and why.

The viewer shows memory packets, code nodes, risks, and review queues. Use it when you need to trust what was retrieved.

", "originalFile": " Kage — verified repo knowledge for coding agents One package. Any MCP-capable coding agent.

Kage ships a stdio MCP server and a CLI. kage install auto-detects and wires every agent on your machine; kage setup <agent> --write wires on {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/index.html", "newString": "

One package. Any MCP-capable coding agent.

Kage ships a stdio MCP server and a CLI. kage install auto-detects and wires every agent on your machine; kage setup <agent> --write wires one by hand. Claude Code users can also /plugin marketplace add kage-core/Kage.

", "oldString": "

One package. Any MCP-capable coding agent.

Kage ships a stdio MCP server and a CLI. Run kage setup <agent> --write once per repo.

v2.2 — memory that follows you Personal memory in ~/.kage/memory with kage sync over your own git remote; conflicts resolve newes {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/releases.html", "newString": "
v2.2 — memory that follows you Personal memory in ~/.kage/memory with kage sync over your own git remote; conflicts resolve newest-wins with both versions preserved. Plus the kage merge-packet git driver, a signal gate that keeps junk out of auto-capture, and kage audit-claude-mem — a read-only truth report over an existing claude-mem store.
v2.1 — the session loop closes itself Auto-distill captures sessions the agent forgot to save (drafts, reviewed by you); kage resume opens each session with a "previously…" digest; kage repair fixes broken packets and indexes in one command; <private>…</private> spans are never stored.
v2.0 — verified knowledge + receipts The Truth Report (kage scan), the per-repo value ledger behind kage gains

kage install One-shot setup

Init + index + auto-detects installed agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Goose, Aider) and wires them. The fastest path from zero to live {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/guide.html", "newString": "

kage install One-shot setup

Init + index + auto-detects installed agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Goose, Aider) and wires them. The fastest path from zero to live memory.

npx -y @kage-core/kage-graph-mcp install

kage scan Truth Report

Zero-setup, ~60-second report on any repo: duplicate implementations, ghost exports, bus-factor-1 hot files, knowledge voids, and doc lies — every finding cites file:line evidence.

kage scan --project . [--json]

kage gains Savings receipt

What the memory harness actually did: tokens and dollars saved (measured, per-packet replay value), stale memories blocked, stale knowledge caught at change-time.

kage gains --project .

kage resume Session continuity

The "previously…" digest: last session's work, distilled learnings, drafts awaiting review, unresolved reconciliation, and - /Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/guide.html: {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/guide.html", "newString": "

", "originalFile": " Kage Docs kage://docs

Kage docs.

Kage is a CLI and MCP server that gives AI coding agents repo-local memory. Stored as reviewable JSON packets connected to code, recalled before future edits. This g {"file": {"content": "

kage://docs

Kage docs.

Kage is a CLI and MCP server that gives AI coding agents repo-local memory. Stored as reviewable JSON packets connected to code, recalled before future edits. This guide gets you from npm install to a working setup, then explains the concepts and reference.

Quickstart

", "filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/guide.html", "numLines": 12, "startLine": 176, "totalLines": 590}, "type": "text"} - /Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/guide.html: {"file": {"content": "

First-day workflow

Use your coding agent normally. Kage is meant to feel ambient.

Before work

Agents call kage_context (MCP) or kage recall (CLI) to load relevant memory and code graph facts. {"file": {"content": "

First-day workflow

Use your coding agent normally. Kage is meant to feel ambient.

Before work

Agents call kage_context (MCP) or kage recall (CLI) to load relevant memory and code graph facts.

kage recall "how does auth work?" --project . --explain

After learning

Capture decisions, gotchas, setup commands, and code explanations as concise packets.

kage learn --project . --type gotcha \
 --learning "Do not merge these retry paths; one uses idempotency keys."

After meaningful changes

Refresh generated artifacts so recall, graph, metrics, and stale checks match the current tree.

kage refresh --project .

Before handoff

Create branch change memory from the diff and run merge readiness.

kage propose --project . --from-diff
kage pr check --project . --json

Optional automation

Install a marker- - /Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/guide.html: {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/guide.html", "newString": "

First-day workflow

Use your coding agent normally — with the hooks installed, the loop below runs itself. Every command here is the manual equivalent of something Kage already does ambiently.

First-day workflow

Use your coding agent normally — with the hooks installed, the loop below runs itself. Every command here is the manual equivalent of something Kage already does ambiently.

Sessions start warm

The SessionStart hook injects the memory policy plus a "previously…" digest and recent-memory timeline. When the agent reads a file, verified packets citing it are injected right then.

kage resume --project . # what the hook injects
kage recall "how does auth work?" --project .

Learnings become packets — even forgotten ones

Agents capture explicitly with kage_learn; sessions that saved nothing are auto-distilled into pending drafts at session end (signal-gated, reviewed by you).

kage learn --project . --type gotcha \
 --learning "Do not merge these retry paths; one uses idempotency keys."
kage inbox --project . # review auto-distilled drafts

Your diff gets checked

kage_profile

Compact project orientation for agents: repo shape, top concepts, key files, commands, memory focus, and next actions.

kage_capabilities

Evidence-backed readiness report across mem {"file": {"content": "

kage_profile

Compact project orientation for agents: repo shape, top concepts, key files, commands, memory focus, and next actions.

kage_capabilities

Evidence-backed readiness report across memory, collaboration, benchmark proof, and dashboard/viewer proof.

kage_context_slots, kage_context_slot_set, kage_context_slot_delete

List, upsert, or delete pinned repo context slots that are included before task-specific recall.

kage_session_replay

Review observed agent session timelines, touched paths, commands, durable candidates, and distill actions without raw transcript text.

kage_reviewers, kage_contributors

Local git-derived reviewer suggestions and contributor profiles.

kage_decisions, kage_module_health, kage_graph_insights

Why-memory coverage, module scorecards, arc - /Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/guide.html: {"filePath": "/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/docs/guide.html", "newString": "kage_learn (or kage propose --from-diff)", "oldString": "kage_learn (or kage propose-from-diff)", "originalFile": " kage_learn (or kage propose --from-diff)", "oldString": "kage_learn (or kage propose-from-diff)", "originalFile": " Kage Docs
